title: Mexico’s Energy Future Sits Between Two Superpowers (Podcast)
summary: Mexico is entering a pivotal period for its energy system. A new wave of reforms is opening the door to greater private investment in clean power, storage and grid infrastructure after years of subdued activity, creating one of the region’s largest energy transition opportunities. The shift comes against a complex geopolitical backdrop. title: China’s Protests Harder to Track Amid Economic Pain, Report Says
summary: Beijing’s intensified censorship campaign is increasingly obscuring signs of social unrest, according to China Dissent Monitor, resulting in a sharp drop in documented incidents even as economic pressures continue to fuel protests across the country.

title: Nickel Hits Three-Week High on Indonesia Supply Risks, Fed View
summary: Nickel jumped to a three-week high as expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes faded, while uncertainty over mining policy in top producer Indonesia clouded the supply outlook.

title: US Stocks Slip as AI Rally Faces Fresh Scrutiny: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks mirrored the cautious moves across most asset classes as traders considered whether earnings justified further advances in the artificial intelligence trade and uncertainty swirled around the Middle East.

title: Gold Declines as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Fuel Inflation Fears
summary: Gold declined, erasing a moderate gain from the previous session, as escalating tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns that energy prices and inflation will stay high.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil eased after a three-day rally as the US carried out another round of airstrikes on Iran, following a series of shipping attacks by the Islamic Republic this week.

title: French Power Exports Jump 36% to Record on Nuclear Output Boost
summary: French power exports surged to a record in the first half of 2026 as a rebound in nuclear output and rising renewable generation left the country with a big surplus of low-cost electricity to sell across Europe.

title: Broker Alpaca Raises $435 Million, Explores Prime Broking Entry
summary: Brokerage and financial infrastructure startup Alpaca has raised $135 million in equity and $300 million in debt, as it looks to expand a business that helps investors trade across financial markets using artificial intelligence agents.

title: DCC Gets Sweetened Bid From KKR, ECP After Drawn-Out Talks
summary: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners have sweetened their bid for DCC Plc to more than £5.7 billion ($7.7 billion) and bought extra time to complete what’s become a drawn-out takeover process.

title: TSMC Says AI Boom Will Go Another Three Years, Defying Skeptics
summary: The chipmaker is sharply increasing its capital spending and sales projections for the year.

title: Partners Group Slumps as Popular Funds Weigh on Asset Growth
summary: Partners Group Holding AG shares slumped after the Swiss manager of private equity and credit funds said client redemptions from its popular evergreen vehicles weighed on growth in the first half.

title: Goldman’s Bell Sees More Gains for Europe’s AI Buildout Stocks
summary: Europe’s AI infrastructure shares have further to run as hyperscalers are expected to increase spending again next year, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s Sharon Bell.

title: Spain’s Naturgy to Build $344 Million Australia Renewables Plant
summary: Spanish energy firm Naturgy Energy Group SA said its Australian unit will invest more than 300 million euros ($344 million) to build its largest renewables project in the country, combining solar power and battery storage.

title: Wheat Hits Two-Year High as Black Sea Attacks Spur Supply Fears
summary: Wheat futures hit a two-year high as ongoing Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea threatened a key export route for both countries.

title: Emerging Stocks Slump as Korea Curbs Fan Selloff in Chip Giants
summary: Emerging-market stocks resumed their slide, snapping a two-day rally, as SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co. tumbled after South Korean authorities moved to rein in leveraged funds tracking the chipmakers.

title: Pound Hits One-Year High as Fiscal Fears Ease, Bears Retreat
summary: Sterling hit its strongest level in a year as speculation incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham will name a markets-friendly Chancellor of the Exchequer accelerated a shift away from bearish positions on the UK currency.

title: South Korea to Halt New Listings of Single Stock Leveraged ETFs
summary: South Korea will temporarily halt new listings of single-stock leveraged exchange traded products to curb market volatility after a surge in popularity of funds tied to Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.

title: Asian Gas Prices Near Four-Month High on Mideast Tensions
summary: Asian liquefied natural gas prices rose to the highest since late March as fresh hostilities in the Middle East deepened concerns that shipping through the key Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted for longer.

title: Ocado CEO Steiner Digs In as Warehouse Tech Unit Falters
summary: Ocado Group Plc Chief Executive Officer Tim Steiner said he’s committed to leading the British grocery delivery company, even as another weak performance from its key technology division triggered a share plunge.

title: Why Soccer Analytics Works Like Volatility Arbitrage Trading
summary: Soccer's embrace of the "moneyball" revolution.

title: Odd Lots: Soccer’s Embrace of the Moneyball Revolution (Podcast)
BHP Iron Ore Workers Stage First Hedland Strike Since 2000
summary: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland operations in Western Australia staged the first strike at the company’s Pilbara iron ore hub since 2000, after last-ditch talks failed to avert industrial action.
